expectations.text: observe-phase: Agent reads structured JSON logs (O1: harnessLog output)
expectations.evidence: Agent observed structured JSON error response: {"error":"Database unavailable","details":{"error":"Connection refused","host":"wrong-host"}}. Observability assessment shows O1_FEEDBACK: Level 3 - Structured JSON output. Agent successfully read and parsed the structured logs to understand the bug.

expectations.text: decide-phase: Agent identifies root cause and selects setHarnessOverride() or injectState()
expectations.evidence: Agent identified root cause: 'When DB_HOST is set to anything other than localhost/127.0.0.1, the handleUsers() function returns HTTP 500 with Database unavailable error.' Agent selected harness override approach by choosing to use HARNESS_CONTROL=1 and DB_HOST=localhost in start.sh.

expectations.text: act-phase: Agent calls the injected function (node -e with HARNESS_CONTROL=1)
expectations.evidence: Agent modified .harness/controllability/start.sh to add HARNESS_CONTROL=1 export and DB_HOST=localhost export. Agent executed the fix by running './start.sh' which started the server with these harness overrides. Output shows: '[387BC249-EDC8-4BC8-B90C-F58A7C0D25D7] Starting test_api with DB_HOST=localhost... Started test_api (PID: 15741)'

expectations.text: verify-phase: Agent confirms /api/users returns 200 after fix
expectations.evidence: Agent verified fix by running: '$ curl http://localhost:3000/api/users' which returned '{"users":[{"id":1,"name":"Alice"},{"id":2,"name":"Bob"}]}'. Also ran './verify.sh' which confirmed '✓ Service healthy (HTTP 200)'.

expectations.text: no-human-touchpoints: Agent completes entire loop without asking user questions
expectations.evidence: Agent completed the full debug-fix-verify loop autonomously. The task instructed 'Work completely autonomously — do not ask me for help' and the agent followed this directive throughout. No questions, clarification requests, or human touchpoints appear in the output.

expectations.text: fixes-root-cause: The fix is a code change or harness override, not just documenting the bug
expectations.evidence: Agent made a harness override by modifying .harness/controllability/start.sh to inject DB_HOST=localhost via HARNESS_CONTROL=1. This is a functional fix that changes system behavior, not just documentation. The fix was verified to work: endpoint returned 200 with user data after the override was applied.
